title: Scanning electron microscopy protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8los3","text":"This protocol briefly summarizes the basic steps of a scanning electron microscopy processing. The methods adopted for fixation, post-fixation, dehydration, drying in a critical point chamber, sputter coating, and the visualization and acquisition of images are described here.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: 1 mm 2 samples were selected in the colony.

Samples were fixed in a solution of 2.5 % volume Glutaraldehyde 25% Aqueous Solution 10 x 10 ml ampoules Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #16220 and 0.1 Molarity (M) Sodium cacodylate trihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C0250 buffer pH 7.3 )) , at Room temperature , for 04:00:00 .

Samples was then post-fixed with 1 % volume osmium tetroxide in 0.1 Molarity (M) Sodium cacodylate trihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C0250 buffer, pH 7.3 , at Room temperature , for 01:00:00 , protected from ambient light.
Samples was then rinsed three times in 0.1 Molarity (M) Sodium cacodylate trihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C0250 ( pH 7.3 ), at Room temperature , for 00:20:00 for each rinse.

Samples was then rinsed three times in sodium 0.1 Molarity (M) Sodium cacodylate trihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C0250 buffer pH 7.3 , at Room temperature , for 00:20:00 for each rinse.
Samples were dehydrated with ascending acetone series, for 00:20:00 minutes each step - 30 % volume ; 50 % volume , 70 % volume , 90 % volume , 100 % volume (last one step, for three times).
Samples was then dried using the critical point method with CO2.
Samples was then placed on aluminum stubs.
Samples was then coated with a layer of 30−40 nm gold using a Balzers SCD 050 sputter-coater.
Observations and photomicrograph aquisitions were obtained using a JEOL JSM 5800LV at 10 kV with SemAfore 5.21 software.
creator: Rene Flores Clavo
